title: Kinetic energy
short: The energy of motion, E = ½·m·v², measured in joules (J).
full:
Kinetic energy is the energy an object has because it is moving, E = ½·m·v². Unlike momentum it is a scalar (no direction) and it grows with the square of the speed — doubling the speed quadruples the kinetic energy.

The work–energy theorem connects it to force: the net work done on an object equals the change in its kinetic energy. That is why stopping a fast car takes a much longer braking distance than stopping a slow one.

The SI unit is the joule (J); 1 J = 1 kg·m²/s².
